response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS R. THE RADIUS OF THE SECOND CIRCLE IS 1.1R. THE RADIUS OF THE THIRD CIRCLE IS (1.1)²R AND SO ON. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF ALL 8 CIRCLES. THIS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + ... + (1.1)¹⁴). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR² TIMES THE SUM OF A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH 8 TERMS WHERE EACH TERM IS THE SQUARE OF 1.1 RAISED TO THE POWER OF THE TERM NUMBER MINUS ONE. THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS (1.1¹⁶ - 1)/(1.1² - 1). THE TOTAL AREA IS πR²(1.1¹⁶ - 1)/(1.1² - 1). THIS EXPRESSION REPRESENTS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IN TERMS OF R AND π.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR(1.1⁴). THIS IS THE TOTAL LENGTH AVAILABLE TO PLACE TEXT CHARACTERS. EACH CHARACTER IS SEPARATED BY A 0.5 CM GAP. THE TOTAL SPACE REQUIRED FOR EACH CHARACTER AND GAP IS 1.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE INTEGER PART OF THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 1.5. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2πR(1.1⁴). DIVIDING BY 1.5 GIVES THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS. THE RESULT IS THE FLOOR OF (2πR(1.1⁴))/1.5. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER IS AN INTEGER. THIS VALUE REPRESENTS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE TEXT
